hud.noNearbyNpc: LivingNPCs: No nearby NPC to trigger.
skip.notInLocation: the NPC is not on the current map
skip.dailyBudget: this NPC's daily trigger limit has been reached
help.thanksReceived: Thanks, I'll take this for now.
help.reward.friendshipHud: LivingNPCs: Completed {{npc}}'s help request, extra friendship +{{amount}}.
help.reward.inventoryFullMail: LivingNPCs: Your inventory was full, so {{npc}} will mail you {{item}} tomorrow as thanks.
help.reward.giftMailScheduled: LivingNPCs: {{npc}} will mail you {{item}} tomorrow as a thank-you.

{{npc}} asked you to help find something. {{step}}{{detail}}
{{due}}

{{npc}} wanted to ask you about something. {{step}}{{detail}}
{{due}}

help.followUp.questionMail: I thought more about what we talked about last time. I hope the letter reached you.
help.followUp.itemMail: That thing you brought last time really helped. I hope the letter reached you.
help.followUp.questionBigReward: That last conversation helped me sort some things out.
help.followUp.itemBigReward: What you brought last time really helped me.
help.followUp.question: I kept thinking about what you said last time.
help.followUp.item: That thing you brought came in handy.
worldAction.wateredCrops: LivingNPCs: {{npc}} watered {{count}} crop tile(s) for you.
dialogueInfluence.location.beach: After you mentioned the beach, I wanted to come see it too.
dialogueInfluence.location.archaeologyHo: After you mentioned this place, I wanted to stop by.
dialogueInfluence.location.forest: After we talked about the forest, I felt like taking a walk here.
dialogueInfluence.location.mountain: After you mentioned the mountain, I started thinking about this place too.
dialogueInfluence.location.default: After you mentioned {{location}}, I kept thinking about it.
location.flowerDance: the edge of the Flower Dance meadow
gift.mailWhenInventoryFull: LivingNPCs: Your inventory was full, so {{npc}} will mail you {{item}} tomorrow.
gift.hud.preference: LivingNPCs: {{npc}} remembered what you like and gave you {{item}}.
gift.hud.thanks: LivingNPCs: {{npc}} gave you {{item}} as a thank-you.
gift.hud.meaningful: LivingNPCs: {{npc}} gave you a heartfelt gift: {{item}}.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal: Dear @,^I've been thinking about the {{sourceGift}} you gave me. It was kind of you, and it stayed with me more than I expected.^I found {{item}} and thought it might suit you. Please take it as a small return gift. No need to make a big thing of it.^- {{npc}}
gift.mail.body.inventory_full: Dear @,^I tried to hand you {{item}} earlier, but your pack looked full. I didn't want it to get lost in the shuffle, so I sent it along this way instead.^Hope it reaches you in one piece.^- {{npc}}
gift.mail.body.meaningful: Dear @,^Some things are easier to put in a letter than to say while standing face to face.^I wanted you to have {{item}}. It made me think of you, and I hope it carries the feeling better than I can explain.^- {{npc}}
gift.mail.body.thanks: Dear @,^Thank you again for helping me out. I know it might have seemed like a small thing, but it meant a lot to me.^I tucked {{item}} in with this letter as a little thanks.^- {{npc}}
gift.mail.body.preference: Dear @,^I remembered you mentioning things like this, so when I came across {{item}}, I thought of you right away.^Maybe it will be useful, or maybe it will simply brighten the day a little.^- {{npc}}
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward: Dear @,^I wanted to write while the help you gave me was still fresh in my mind. Bringing {{sourceGift}} may have looked like a small errand, but it made the whole request feel cared for instead of forgotten.^I picked out {{item}} as a proper thank-you. Please take it as something chosen with real gratitude, not just a quick reward.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.penny.0: Dear @,^I kept thinking about the {{sourceGift}} you gave me. It made an ordinary day feel carefully noticed.^I found {{item}} and thought it might be useful on the farm. Please accept it as a quiet return for your kindness.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.penny.1: Dear @,^Thank you again for {{sourceGift}}. Small thoughtful things can linger in a person's mind more than expected.^I tucked {{item}} into this letter. It felt like the sort of thing that should go to someone who pays attention.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.penny: Dear @,^Yesterday, after you brought {{sourceGift}}, I was able to finish what I had been setting aside. It sounds simple, but being remembered made the whole task lighter.^I chose {{item}} as a thank-you. I hope it is useful, and I hope you know the help meant more than the errand.

Dear @,^I wanted to write before the feeling faded: you really did help me when you brought {{sourceGift}}. Sometimes a small promise kept is what makes the day easier.^Please take {{item}} with my thanks. It is modest, but chosen with care.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.sebastian.0: Hey @,^I meant to say something about {{sourceGift}} in person, but I got awkward and probably looked like I was thinking about a bug in my code.^Anyway, I found {{item}} and thought you might actually use it. So... thanks.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.sebastian.1: Hey,^The {{sourceGift}} was a good surprise. I do not always know what to do with nice gestures, but I noticed it.^I sent {{item}}. No dramatic meaning attached. Just something that made me think of you.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.sebas: Hey @,^About {{sourceGift}}: thanks for actually following through. I know I can sound like I expect people to forget, so it was... good that you didn't.^I sent {{item}}. It seemed better than trying to say all of that out loud.

Hey,^You helped with {{sourceGift}}, and I appreciated it more than I probably showed. The quiet kind of help counts.^I mailed {{item}}. Use it, sell it, stash it, whatever works. I wanted you to have it.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.abigail.0: Hey @,^I am officially counting {{sourceGift}} as a successful side quest reward. It made my day more interesting, which is not nothing around here.^I found {{item}} and thought it had the right kind of adventure energy for you.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.abigail.1: Hey,^Thanks for {{sourceGift}}. I could pretend I am being totally casual about it, but honestly, it was awesome.^So here is {{item}}. Consider it loot from the friendship dungeon.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.abiga: Hey @,^You actually brought {{sourceGift}}. Quest complete, reputation improved, very nice work.^I sent {{item}} because every good quest needs a reward, and because I really am grateful.
Hey,^I kept thinking about how you helped with {{sourceGift}}. Not everyone follows through when things get inconvenient.^So I mailed {{item}}. It felt like the right kind of treasure to send back.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.alex.0: Hey @,^Thanks for {{sourceGift}}. I know I can act like nothing gets to me, but that was a solid move.^I sent {{item}} back your way. Consider it a return pass, farm style.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.alex.1: Hey,^The {{sourceGift}} was great. Seriously. It kind of made the day feel like a win.^Here is {{item}}. I figured someone who shows up like that deserves something useful.

gift.mail.body.reciprocal.claire.0: Hi @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. I was tired when I received it, so I probably seemed quieter than I meant to.^I sent {{item}} because the gesture stayed with me after the shift ended.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.claire.1: Hi,^The {{sourceGift}} was kind of you. Small unexpected things can make a long commute feel a little less gray.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.clair: Hi @,^Thank you for bringing {{sourceGift}}. It was one less thing hanging over my head, and I cannot tell you how much I needed that.^I mailed {{item}} as a thank-you.
Hi,^You helped with {{sourceGift}}, and I appreciated the quiet reliability of it.^Please take {{item}}. It is not much, but it is sincere.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.sophia.0: Dear @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. I got a little nervous about how to answer, but it made me really happy.^I sent {{item}} back to you. I hope it feels gentle and useful.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.sophia.1: Dear @,^The {{sourceGift}} was so thoughtful. It made the vineyard feel a little less quiet for a while.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks. I chose it carefully.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.sophi: Dear @,^Thank you for bringing {{sourceGift}}. I know I worry too much sometimes, but you helping made the whole thing feel manageable.^I mailed {{item}} as a small thank-you.
Dear @,^You remembered {{sourceGift}}, and that meant a lot to me. It felt like I did not have to carry the request alone.^Please take {{item}}. I hope it brings you comfort.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.susan.0: Hi @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. After being cut off from town for so long, neighborly gestures still mean a great deal to me.^I sent {{item}} in return. Hope it helps on your farm.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.susan.1: Hi,^I appreciated {{sourceGift}}. It had the honest feeling of one farmer looking out for another.^Please take {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.susan: Hi @,^Thanks for bringing {{sourceGift}}. You helped me keep the day moving, and that matters when there is always more work waiting.^I mailed {{item}} as a thank-you.
Hi,^Your help with {{sourceGift}} was very welcome. Reliable neighbors make even hard seasons feel less lonely.^Please accept {{item}} with my gratitude.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.victor.0: Dear @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It was a considerate gesture, and I found myself thinking about the intention behind it.^I sent {{item}} in return. I hope it proves useful.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.victor.1: Dear @,^I appreciated {{sourceGift}}. Sometimes a small act of kindness is more elegantly designed than anything on paper.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.victo: Dear @,^Thank you for bringing {{sourceGift}}. Your timing was excellent, and the whole matter resolved far more cleanly because of it.^I mailed {{item}} as a thank-you.
Dear @,^Your help with {{sourceGift}} was both practical and considerate. I appreciate that combination more than I can easily say.^Please take {{item}} with my gratitude.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.olivia.0: Dear @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It was a gracious surprise, and I do appreciate good taste when it appears unannounced.^I sent {{item}} in return. Please accept it with my warm regards.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.olivia.1: Dear @,^The {{sourceGift}} you gave me had a lovely sense of timing. A good gesture can be as pleasing as a well-poured glass of wine.^Please take {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.olivi: Dear @,^Thank you for bringing {{sourceGift}}. You handled the request with admirable consideration.^I mailed {{item}} as a proper token of appreciation.
Dear @,^Your assistance with {{sourceGift}} was very much appreciated. It is pleasant to be able to rely on someone without making a production of it.^Please accept {{item}} with my gratitude.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.lance.0: Farmer,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. In the field, small supplies and timely gestures can change the shape of a day.^I sent {{item}} in return. May it serve you well.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.lance.1: @,^The {{sourceGift}} was appreciated. You have good instincts for when something will matter.^Please accept {{item}}. Consider it a useful token from one traveler to another.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.lance: Farmer,^You brought {{sourceGift}} as requested. Good follow-through is rare enough that it deserves notice.^I mailed {{item}} as thanks. Keep it close if it proves useful.
@,^Thank you for helping with {{sourceGift}}. You handled the task cleanly, and that speaks well of you.^Please take {{item}} with my respect.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.scarlett.0: Hey @,^Thanks for {{sourceGift}}! That was really sweet of you, and I am not going to pretend it did not make me smile.^I sent {{item}} back. Hope it brightens your day too.

gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.scarl: Hey @,^You brought {{sourceGift}}, and you totally came through. Thank you.^I mailed {{item}} because help like that deserves a real thank-you.
Hey,^Thanks for helping with {{sourceGift}}. You made the whole thing easier, and I appreciate it a lot.^Please accept {{item}}. It is from me, with full enthusiasm.

gift.mail.body.reciprocal.gunther.1: Dear @,^I appreciated {{sourceGift}}. Thoughtful exchanges are a kind of living history, small but worth preserving.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks.

gift.mail.body.reciprocal.morris.1: Dear @,^I received {{sourceGift}} and appreciated the gesture. Efficiency is not the only measure of value, though it helps.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.morri: Dear @,^Thank you for bringing {{sourceGift}}. Your assistance resolved the matter promptly and with minimal friction.^I mailed {{item}} as compensation for your time.
Dear @,^Your help with {{sourceGift}} was noted and appreciated. A reliable completion deserves a proper acknowledgment.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.leah.0: Dear @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It had that lovely feeling of something chosen by hand, not just picked up on the way past.^I found {{item}} and thought it might bring a little texture or usefulness to your day.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.leah.1: Dear @,^The {{sourceGift}} you gave me has been sitting in my thoughts like a bright color on a quiet canvas.^I wanted to send {{item}} in return. Nothing grand, just a small shape of gratitude.
Dear @,^When you brought {{sourceGift}}, it helped more than the practical part of the request. It reminded me how much care can live in a small gesture.^I chose {{item}} for you. I hope it feels useful, or at least warmly meant.
Dear @,^I finished what I needed to do because you remembered {{sourceGift}}. There is something beautiful about a promise carried all the way to someone's door.^Please accept {{item}} as my thanks.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.elliott.0: Dear @,^Your gift of {{sourceGift}} was a graceful kindness, the sort that brightens a room after the giver has already left it.^I send {{item}} in return, humble though it is, with sincere appreciation.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.elliott.1: Dear @,^I have been thinking of the {{sourceGift}} you offered me. A well-timed gift can feel almost like a line of poetry arriving where it belongs.^Please accept {{item}} as a small reply.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.ellio: Dear @,^You fulfilled my request for {{sourceGift}} with admirable dependability. Such favors may appear small on paper, but in life they carry real weight.^I enclose {{item}} with my gratitude.
Dear @,^I wished to thank you properly for bringing {{sourceGift}}. Your help turned a lingering concern into a finished chapter.^Please take {{item}} as a modest token of appreciation.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.linus.0: Hello @,^Thank you for sharing {{sourceGift}} with me. A gift given freely has its own warmth.^I am sending {{item}}. It is simple, but I hope it finds a good use with you.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.linus.1: Friend,^I appreciated {{sourceGift}}. Kindness does not need ceremony, but it should still be answered.^Please take {{item}}. May it serve you well.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.linus: Hello @,^You brought {{sourceGift}} when I asked, and that means something. Many people are busy, but you remembered.^I am sending {{item}} as thanks. It is not fancy, only useful and honestly given.
Friend,^The help with {{sourceGift}} eased my day. I do not have much to give, but I know the value of returning kindness.^Please accept {{item}} with my gratitude.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.maru.0: Hi @,^Thanks for {{sourceGift}}. I keep thinking about how useful small well-timed inputs can be in a system, social systems included.^I sent {{item}} because it seemed like a good fit for your farm's current variables.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.maru.1: Hi @,^The {{sourceGift}} was a really thoughtful data point in my day. That sounds too clinical, but I mean it in the best way.^I found {{item}} and thought it might be practically useful.
Hi @,^Bringing {{sourceGift}} solved the problem much faster than my backup plan would have. Thank you for being the most reliable part of that process.^I sent {{item}} as a small return. I hope it is useful.
Hi @,^I wanted to follow up on {{sourceGift}}. Your help let me finish what I was working on without having to improvise a messy workaround.^Please take {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.shane.0: Hey @,^About {{sourceGift}}. I am not great at making a whole speech out of it, but... thanks. Really.^I sent {{item}}. Do not make it weird. Just take it.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.shane.1: Hey,^The {{sourceGift}} was nice. There, I said it. Try not to look too smug.^I mailed {{item}} because it seemed fair, and because I wanted to.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.shane: Hey @,^You brought {{sourceGift}} like you said you would. I know that should be normal, but it still counts.^I sent {{item}}. It is a thank-you, even if I am bad at packaging those.
Hey,^Thanks for helping with {{sourceGift}}. You did not have to bother, but you did, and I noticed.^There is {{item}} in the letter. Use it however you want.
gift.mail.body.default: Dear @,^I saw {{item}} and thought you might find a use for it. It felt better to send it now than to wait until I ran into you.^Take care.^- {{npc}}
help.mail.body.rewardMoney: Dear @,^Thanks again for helping {{npc}} with {{item}}. The town help fund set aside this reward, so please accept it with our appreciation.^- LivingNPCs
outing.start.beach: Come on, let's get some fresh air at the beach.
outing.start.seedShop: Come on, let's go browse the shop.
outing.start.archaeologyHouse: Come on, let's take our time looking around inside.
outing.start.saloon: Come on, let's sit a while at the saloon.
outing.start.mountain: Come on, let's head up to the mountain.
outing.start.forest: Come on, let's take a walk in the forest.
outing.start.farm: Come on, let's spend a little while at the farm.
outing.start.flowerDance: Come on, let's go see the flower field.
outing.start.scenic: Come on, let's go enjoy the view over there.
outing.start.browse: Come on, let's go browse for a bit.
outing.start.quiet: Come on, let's find somewhere quieter.
outing.arrival.seedShop: The shop is quiet enough right now to take our time.
outing.arrival.archaeologyHouse: Now that it has quieted down, it's a nice place to look around slowly.
outing.arrival.saloon: The lively hum in here is kind of comforting.
outing.arrival.busStop: When the wind blows through here, it's a nice spot to pause.
outing.arrival.town: The town isn't too noisy at this hour.
outing.arrival.hospital: It's better to keep things quiet in here.
outing.arrival.browse: We can take our time looking around here.
outing.arrival.quiet: It's just the right kind of quiet here.
outing.arrival.default: This is a nice place to stay a while.
outing.returnOpening.afternoon: It's about time we headed back,
outing.return.beach: {{opening}}we've had our fill of the sea breeze.
outing.return.archaeologyHouse: {{opening}}we can look at the books and exhibits another time.
outing.return.saloon: {{opening}}any longer and we'd lose track of time.
outing.return.mountain: {{opening}}we've lingered on the mountain long enough.
outing.return.forest: {{opening}}the forest is starting to grow still.
outing.return.farm: {{opening}}it's been really pleasant here at the farm.
outing.return.flowerDance: {{opening}}we should leave the breeze by the flowers for others now.
outing.return.scenic: {{opening}}we've taken in the view to our heart's content.
gmcm.para.loaded: LivingNPCs is loaded. If you can see this page, the mod is running.
gmcm.para.commands: SMAPI debug commands: livingnpcs_debug, livingnpcs_prompt, livingnpcs_export, livingnpcs_eval, livingnpcs_restore_gift_mail.
gmcm.enableMod.name: Enable LivingNPCs
gmcm.enableMod.tooltip: Changes take full effect after a game restart.
gmcm.behaviorHotkey.name: Behavior hotkey
gmcm.behaviorHotkey.tooltip: Press this key near an NPC to trigger one small NPC behavior.
gmcm.inspectMemoryHotkey.name: Inspect-memory hotkey
gmcm.inspectMemoryHotkey.tooltip: Press this key near an NPC to print that NPC's LivingNPCs behavior memory to the SMAPI console.
gmcm.debug.name: Debug logging
gmcm.debug.tooltip: Print more LivingNPCs debug info to the SMAPI console.
gmcm.showHud.name: Show trigger messages
gmcm.showHud.tooltip: After a manual trigger, show the result or failure reason in the bottom-left corner.
gmcm.manualMode.name: Manual behavior mode
gmcm.manualMode.tooltip: Choose which behavior the hotkey always tests. Auto picks one by the rules.
gmcm.manualEmote.name: Manual emote id
gmcm.manualEmote.tooltip: Used when the manual behavior mode is Emote. 16 is usually the exclamation mark.
gmcm.maxMemory.name: Memories kept per NPC
gmcm.maxMemory.tooltip: How many LivingNPCs behavior memories each NPC saves to the save file at most.
gmcm.promptMemory.name: Memories sent to ValleyTalk
gmcm.promptMemory.tooltip: When building context, how many recent behavior memories to send to ValleyTalk at most.
gmcm.conversationMemory.name: Record conversation starts
gmcm.conversationMemory.tooltip: When you click an NPC to chat, record a light interaction memory and send context to ValleyTalk early.
gmcm.helpRequests.name: Enable NPC help requests
gmcm.helpRequests.tooltip: Allow NPCs to ask the player for one light item favor during AI conversation when the relationship feels natural.
gmcm.maxPendingHelp.name: Max pending help requests per NPC
gmcm.maxPendingHelp.tooltip: Limit how many unfinished help requests a single NPC can have at once.
gmcm.helpCooldown.name: Help request cooldown (days)
gmcm.helpCooldown.tooltip: Minimum number of days between two new help requests from the same NPC.
gmcm.helpMinTrust.name: Min trust for help requests
gmcm.helpMinTrust.tooltip: An NPC only opens up to ask for help once relationship trust reaches this value.
gmcm.helpDailyChance.name: Daily help-request chance
gmcm.helpDailyChance.tooltip: When you first talk to an NPC with at least 2 hearts each day, the chance they bring up a small favor on their own. 0 disables proactive asking.
gmcm.helpMinReward.name: Min friendship reward on completion
gmcm.helpMinReward.tooltip: The least extra friendship granted for completing an NPC help request.
gmcm.helpMaxReward.name: Max friendship reward on completion
gmcm.helpMaxReward.tooltip: The most extra friendship granted for completing an NPC help request.
gmcm.aiFriendship.name: AI conversation bonus friendship
gmcm.aiFriendship.tooltip: Let AI conversations grant extra friendship by chat quality, on top of vanilla dialogue friendship, capped per NPC per day.
gmcm.aiFriendshipCap.name: Daily AI bonus friendship cap
gmcm.aiFriendshipCap.tooltip: The most bonus friendship an NPC can gain per day from AI conversation. Vanilla dialogue friendship does not count toward this.
gmcm.dialogueFollowUps.name: Dialogue follow-up bubbles
gmcm.dialogueFollowUps.tooltip: Allow AI conversations to leave a brief follow-up overhead bubble when fitting; companion outings never use it to report movement.
gmcm.dialogueDrivenBehaviors.name: Dialogue-driven behaviors
gmcm.dialogueDrivenBehaviors.tooltip: Allow AI conversations, under strict limits, to influence an NPC's small behaviors over the next minutes to days, such as being more willing to approach, keeping distance, or responding naturally at a mentioned place.
gmcm.behaviorInfluenceDays.name: Max behavior-influence days
gmcm.behaviorInfluenceDays.tooltip: How many days a dialogue-driven behavior influence lasts at most. After it expires it no longer affects the NPC.
gmcm.aiWorldActions.name: Allow AI world actions
gmcm.aiWorldActions.tooltip: Allow AI, under strict limits, to trigger a few real effects such as small gifts, a little money, watering, or companion outings.
gmcm.smallGifts.name: Allow NPC small gifts
gmcm.smallGifts.tooltip: Allow NPCs with at least 5 hearts to occasionally give the player a low-value small gift.
gmcm.meaningfulGifts.name: Allow NPC meaningful gifts
gmcm.meaningfulGifts.tooltip: Only with a deep relationship, a recent special event, or a conversation that touches an important long-term memory may an NPC give a more heartfelt gift.
gmcm.meaningfulCooldown.name: Meaningful gift cooldown (days)
gmcm.meaningfulCooldown.tooltip: Minimum days between two meaningful gifts from the same NPC.
gmcm.giftChanceMin.name: 5-heart daily gift chance (min)
gmcm.giftChanceMin.tooltip: After an NPC reaches 5 hearts, the lower bound of the chance to naturally offer a small gift in the first fitting conversation each day.
gmcm.giftChanceMax.name: 5-heart daily gift chance (max)
gmcm.giftChanceMax.tooltip: After an NPC reaches 5 hearts, the upper bound of the chance to naturally offer a small gift in the first fitting conversation each day.
gmcm.moneyGifts.name: Allow NPC money gifts
gmcm.moneyGifts.tooltip: Only when fairly close may an NPC occasionally give the player a little money.
gmcm.moneyCap.name: Max money per gift
gmcm.moneyCap.tooltip: The most gold an AI money gift gives the player at once.
gmcm.farmHelp.name: Allow NPC watering help
gmcm.farmHelp.tooltip: Only on the farm and when fairly close may an NPC water nearby planted-but-unwatered crops.
gmcm.waterTiles.name: Max tiles watered per action
gmcm.waterTiles.tooltip: How many nearby crop tiles one AI watering action affects at most.
gmcm.companionOutings.name: Allow companion outings
gmcm.companionOutings.tooltip: Allow an NPC to accept an invitation to wander somewhere, enjoy the view, or sit quietly for a while, temporarily stepping away from the day's schedule.
gmcm.outingStay.name: Minimum outing stay
gmcm.outingStay.tooltip: How many game minutes the NPC stays after arriving; default 120 minutes, i.e. two game hours.
gmcm.festival.name: Allow festival interactions
gmcm.festival.tooltip: Allow AI to trigger light special interactions during festivals or events.
gmcm.questAssist.name: Allow quest assistance
gmcm.questAssist.tooltip: Allow AI to lightly assist around the player's current quests, without completing them or changing their state.
gmcm.npcState.name: Enable NPC state
gmcm.npcState.tooltip: Track each NPC's light current state, such as mood, attentiveness, and response leaning.
gmcm.stateDecay.name: Daily state decay
gmcm.stateDecay.tooltip: How far NPC state falls back toward normal at the start of each day.
gmcm.emotionDecay.name: Daily emotion decay
gmcm.emotionDecay.tooltip: How far long-term interpersonal emotion falls back toward calm at the start of each day.
gmcm.conflictDecay.name: Daily conflict decay
gmcm.conflictDecay.tooltip: How far unresolved conflict severity naturally drops at the start of each day; apologies and fitting gifts speed recovery further.
gmcm.passive.name: Passive behaviors
gmcm.passive.tooltip: Allow nearby NPCs to react naturally now and then without pressing the hotkey.
gmcm.passiveChance.name: Passive trigger chance
gmcm.passiveChance.tooltip: The chance to trigger a passive behavior every 10 in-game minutes.
gmcm.maxBehaviors.name: Max behaviors per NPC per day
gmcm.maxBehaviors.tooltip: Limit how many times LivingNPCs can trigger a behavior for each NPC per day.
gmcm.interactionDistance.name: Interaction distance
gmcm.interactionDistance.tooltip: The maximum distance, in tiles, at which LivingNPCs can affect a nearby NPC.
gmcm.allowFace.name: Allow facing the player
gmcm.allowFace.tooltip: Allow NPCs to turn toward the player.
gmcm.allowEmote.name: Allow emotes
gmcm.allowEmote.tooltip: Allow NPCs to show emote bubbles.
gmcm.allowApproach.name: Allow walking over
gmcm.allowApproach.tooltip: Allow NPCs to pathfind a short distance to a safe tile beside the player.
gmcm.sve.name: Enable SVE compatibility
gmcm.sve.tooltip: When off, LivingNPCs no longer uses SVE-specific profiles, expansion progress, or expansion NPC context.
gmcm.bridge.name: Send behavior context to ValleyTalk
gmcm.bridge.tooltip: Let ValleyTalk's dialogue generation take an NPC's recent LivingNPCs behavior into account.
gmcm.concisePrompt.name: Concise prompt context
gmcm.concisePrompt.tooltip: Send ValleyTalk a slimmer hidden context: keeps mood, memory, relationship and situational cues (gifts/help/outings), but drops the repeated world-stage and reply-guidance text to save tokens. The AI's response format is unaffected. Off by default.
gmcm.aiPlanner.name: Enable AI behavior planner
gmcm.aiPlanner.tooltip: Optional. Use an OpenAI-compatible endpoint to choose NPC behavior intents.
gmcm.plannerEndpoint.name: Planner endpoint
gmcm.plannerModel.name: Planner model
gmcm.plannerModel.tooltip: The model name used to choose NPC behavior intents.
gmcm.plannerTimeout.name: Planner timeout
gmcm.plannerTimeout.tooltip: After the AI planner times out, it falls back to rule-based behavior. Unit: seconds.
gmcm.manualMode.approachPlayer: Test only: walk over to player
